thinking.text:
The script is designed but still has issues. Let me think about this more carefully.

The problem is that the OCR captures the transcript at 1fps, and each frame shows a scrolling history. Commands that were executed early in the game scroll off screen and are no longer visible in later frames. So I need to capture commands from each frame as they appear.

Better approach:
1. For each frame, get the transcript commands (all `>` lines except the last one being typed)
2. Track the last command in the transcript (the most recently completed one)
3. When this last command changes between frames, it means a new command was completed

This is what I was trying with the "second-to-last" approach, but it didn't work well because the transcript might stay the same across frames (if the frame rate is faster than typing).

At 1fps, the typing of a single command might span multiple frames. The key insight is:
- Frame N shows: [history commands..., current_typing_A]
- Frame N+1 shows: [history commands..., current_typing_B]
- When current_typing changes from A to B, command A was completed.

Let me look at this more carefully with the data I have.

Actually, looking at the OCR outputs, I see that each frame shows a different set of commands in the transcript because the text scrolls. The transcript commands are the commands that have been executed. The last command in the transcript (second-to-last `>` line) is the most recently completed command.

Let me check: in frame_0012.txt, the commands are:
- >n
- >n
- >u (last is being typed)

So the transcript has: n, n (and u is being typed)

In frame_0013.txt, the commands are:
- >n
- >n
- >u
- >get (last is being typed)

So the transcript has: n, n, u (get is being typed)

So frame 12 shows: [n, n] in transcript, [u] typing
Frame 13 shows: [n, n, u] in transcript, [get] typing

The new command in transcript is 'u', which means 'u' was just completed.

So my extraction of the second-to-last command should work, but it only captures when a command is freshly completed (appears for the first time in the transcript).

Let me run a more careful analysis.
